data.title: Leveraged Bitcoin Longs on Bitfinex Weakest Since December and It Could Mean Rally Time
data.description:
• Leveraged bitcoin longs on Bitfinex have dropped to their lowest since December, suggesting a possible bullish trend for Bitcoin.
  • Contrary indicators in financial markets can often signal the opposite of their apparent direction.
  • Historically, increases in Bitfinex long positions have coincided with bitcoin price declines, while decreases have aligned with price rises.

data.title: Bitcoin Life Insurance Firm Meanwhile Shares First Audit
data.description:
• Meanwhile, a Bermuda-based life insurance firm, has released its 2024 audited financial statements, making it one of the first companies fully denominated in bitcoin to do so.
  • The firm operates by having clients pay and receive life insurance payouts in bitcoin, with payments unaffected by shifts in the dollar value of BTC.
  • Meanwhile's audit revealed total assets of 220.4 BTC and a net income of 25.29 BTC for the year ending December 31, 2024.

data.title: Core Foundation, Bitcoin Layer 1 With Over $500M Staked BTC, Adds BitGo and KODA as Validators
data.description:
• Core Foundation has announced BitGo and KODA as new validators, enhancing institutional adoption of Bitcoin-native DeFi.
  • BitGo's involvement brings regulatory credibility, while KODA's integration expands Core's presence in Asia.
  • The Core blockchain uses a hybrid consensus mechanism to combine Proof of Work and Proof of Stake, ensuring security and scalability.

data.title: NEAR Surges 5% After Forming Bullish Support Pattern
data.description:
• Technical analysis of NEAR points towards signs of accumulation.
  • The $2.42 level has been established as support while resistance is at $2.47.

data.title: ATOM Rebounds From 5% Drop as Buyers Defend Key Support Level
data.description:
• ATOM experienced a 5% price decline before finding strong support at the $4.25 level, triggering a recovery rally.
  • Escalating US-China trade tensions and potential ECB rate cuts created mixed signals across cryptocurrency markets.
  • High-volume buying at support levels suggests institutional or whale accumulation despite broader market uncertainty.

data.title: Leverage Reconfigures in Q1: DeFi Recovers, CeFi Quietly Expands, Treasury Debt Mounts
data.description:
• DeFi borrowing grew by more than 30% by May after a first-quarter slump.
  • The increase was driven by capital-efficient assets like Pendle on Aave.
  • Bitcoin treasury firms now hold over $12.7 billion in debt-backed BTC, introducing long-dated credit risk into the crypto market structure.

data.title: Investment Advisors Become Top Holders of Spot Bitcoin ETFs, Ether ETF Demand Rises
data.description:
• Investment advisors now hold over $10.28B in spot Bitcoin ETFs nearly half of total institutional assets suggesting accelerating mainstream adoption.
  • Ether ETF exposure tops $1B, with advisors and hedge funds leading allocations, highlighting a widening institutional appetite beyond Bitcoin.

data.title: Bitcoin's 50-Day Average Hits Record High, but There's a Catch
data.description:
• Bitcoin's 50-day simple moving average reached a record high, indicating a bullish market outlook.
  • However, the narrowing spread between bitcoin's price and the 50-day SMA suggests a potential price correction.
  • Recent data shows increased profit-taking by holders, aligning with the caution indicated by the price-to-SMA spread.

data.title: Crypto Investment Firms 3iQ, Cryptonite Debut Structured Investment Vehicle in Switzerland
data.description:
• AMCs are regulated investment vehicles that allow qualified investors to invest in actively managed products.
  • The new structured product is due to trade on Swiss Exchange SIX.

data.title: Elon Musk Blasts U.S. Spending Bill as Debt Nears $37T
data.description:
• Elon Musk said the bill marks “the largest increase in the debt ceiling in US history” and predicts long-term fiscal damage.
  • The $1.2 trillion package includes tax cuts, defense boosts and social spending reductions — with a projected $2.4 trillion deficit impact.
